characters.physical_details: Short, broad-shouldered, silver-haired; work-swollen hands and a stiff left thumb.
characters.occupation_status: Valley midwife; paid county burial witness and cemetery worker.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English well enough to keep detailed private records.

state_transitions.change: Hired for Old Sion relocation.
Makes selective copies before the ledger is stolen.
Contests Rafe's Eli affidavit.
Names Gabriel publicly.
Gives June the copied pages but keeps Gabriel's page.

characters.availability: Present throughout; pain and exhaustion worsen from chapter 12.

characters.physical_details: Tall, wiry, sun-dark, chin-scarred, with labor-worn hands.
characters.occupation_status: Condemned-land tenant farmer; later Marlowe Dam laborer.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads ordinary English slowly and can manage forms and debt paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Presses Della to name the grave as Eli.
His Eli affidavit becomes active county paperwork.
Starts dam work.
Learns Gabriel was his father and burns his withdrawn affidavit copy.
Publicly remains a Bledsoe.

characters.availability: Present throughout; often off-page at the dam from chapter 12.

characters.physical_details: Lean, quick-moving, with red laundress hands and repeatedly mended clothes.
characters.occupation_status: Laundress, mender, and household manager; gossip hurts her paying work after chapter 7.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads household and church English and writes short notes.

state_transitions.change: Loses Quillen mending work after the child-row scandal.
Pushes Della toward selective disclosure.
Learns Gabriel fathered Rafe and Eli fled after killing him.
Runs food and triage during the final move.

characters.availability: Present in domestic scenes and cemetery support; not central at court.

characters.physical_details: Long-limbed, observant, often ink-stained, child-looking until she speaks exactly.
characters.occupation_status: Schoolgirl-copyist who starts keeping cemetery notes and inherits Della's selected pages.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English well for her age.

state_transitions.change: Overhears Gabriel's name.
Copies grave-relevant extracts.
Learns the paternity truth.
Starts a cemetery notebook that separates confirmed facts from uncertainties.
Takes custody of the copied pages.

characters.availability: Present in household, church, cemetery support, and copy work; absent from sworn testimony.

characters.physical_details: Pinned hair, mud-ruined sensible shoes, official composure.
characters.occupation_status: County registrar for tags, forms, and hearings.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes formal English fluently.

state_transitions.change: Recruits Della as a burial witness.
Receives Rafe's affidavit and starts the contest clock.
Issues a provisional Gabriel identification and refuses an Eli tag.
Carries Gabriel's correction and Noreen's standing into record.

characters.availability: Present at cemetery, office, courthouse, and final move; absent from private Bledsoe scenes.

characters.physical_details: Tall but stooped, with dust-ground hands and a quarry cough.

key_relationships.rafe_bledsoe: elder who knew him from infancy
key_relationships.eli_bledsoe: fugitive husband he helped send off

characters.occupation_status: Old Sion stonemason and grave digger, physically failing but still working.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads simple English and writes his name.

state_transitions.change: Confirms Gabriel's grave can still be identified.
Corroborates Gabriel publicly.
Helps clear the last row and dies that night.

characters.physical_details: Broad, well-kept, church-respectable, used to being believed.
characters.occupation_status: Wolfe tract controller and church deacon whose authority rests on paperwork and respectability.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads deeds, church books, and letters fluently.

state_transitions.change: Orchard evidence undercuts his tract story.
Gabriel's identification breaks his disappearance narrative.
Noreen wins standing against his tract handling.

characters.availability: Present at hearings, church events, and Wolfe-tract scenes; absent from intimate Bledsoe aftermath.

characters.physical_details: Thin-faced, sharp-eyed, in carefully repaired clothes.

key_relationships.vernon_wolfe: brother who benefited from her exclusion

characters.occupation_status: Boardinghouse cleaner/cook living on room wages while pressing her tract claim.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads ordinary English and writes slow letters.

state_transitions.change: Sees that Della's testimony can move property.
Gets map evidence of her exclusion.
Wins standing to challenge the tract.

characters.availability: Present in hearings, church gatherings, and cemetery scenes tied to inheritance.

characters.physical_details: Remembered as handsome; identifiable by healed left collarbone break and missing upper left premolar.
characters.occupation_status: Dead before the story opens; formerly the missing Wolfe orchard heir.
characters.literacy_languages: In life he read and wrote English and handled orchard papers.

state_transitions.change: His unmarked grave is exposed.
His remains are publicly identified.
He is reburied under truthful naming.

characters.availability: Dead before chapter 1; present only in memory, remains, and consequences.

characters.physical_details: Remembered as broad-shouldered, flat-nosed, guarded, with two mill-stiffened fingers.

key_relationships.gabriel_wolfe: rival in the fatal kitchen fight
key_relationships.silas_wynn: witness to the aftermath of his flight

characters.occupation_status: Missing before the story opens; former farm and mill laborer without legal death proof.
characters.literacy_languages: Limited but functional English for forms, wages, and his name.

state_transitions.change: Becomes the false identity Rafe tries to attach to the grave.
His killing of Gabriel and flight are disclosed.
Remains legally unconfirmed as dead, leaving the Bledsoe claim frozen.

characters.availability: Missing before chapter 1 and never appears in present time.

timeline.story_start: Late May 1958, after condemnation notices turn Marlowe Dam from rumor into schedule.
timeline.estimated_span: Late May to the second week of September 1958.

seasonal_track.season: Late spring to early summer; berry season starts; the valley still feels inhabited.
High summer; heat, orchard rot, storms, and humidity dominate work.
Late summer storm season; rain loosens the cemetery slope and deadlines harden.
Early fall; cooler mornings as the new cemetery is used and water rises.

key_events.event: Della is hired for the Old Sion cemetery move.
Sadie Kincaid's exhumation proves grave names redirect compensation and standing.
The fever-plot grave is exposed and Rafe presses for an Eli identification.
key_events.timing: Early summer after rain and blasting

key_events.event: Sadie's hearing proves Della's testimony can move property.
The child-row twin dispute fuels rumor and costs Ruthie paying work.
Della copies grave extracts and the original ledger is stolen overnight.
Rafe's Eli affidavit is revealed and the contest clock begins.
Della recovers Gabriel's pocketwatch and the altered tract map from the Wolfe orchard.
A landslip exposes most of Gabriel's coffin and the dam schedule accelerates.
Gabriel Wolfe is publicly identified at Old Sion.
Della tells Rafe Gabriel fathered him and Eli killed Gabriel before fleeing.
The hearing formally enters Gabriel, confirms the Eli affidavit withdrawn, and grants Noreen standing.
The final cemetery row is moved before gate closure; Silas dies that night.
Gabriel is reburied on Limestone Ridge and Rafe refuses public Wolfe renaming.
The reservoir overtakes the old road, and June takes Della's pages.

geography.primary_setting: Brier Valley, Hale County, Tennessee, below Marlowe Dam on the Red Branch River.

key_locations.name: Old Sion church and cemetery
key_locations.details: Hillside churchyard above the river with leaning markers, cedar roots, and the fever ground.

key_locations.name: Bledsoe cabin on Cedar Bench
key_locations.details: Small uphill house with hearth, cellar, wash porch, valley view.

key_locations.name: Temporary schoolhouse office
key_locations.details: Former classroom turned relocation office for tags, forms, and hearings.

key_locations.name: Wolfe orchard and packing shed
key_locations.details: Neglected fruit land with drowned lower rows, old records, and Gabriel's pocketwatch.

key_locations.name: Larkin courthouse
key_locations.details: County-seat rooms where probate, relocation challenges, and compensation paperwork are decided.

key_locations.name: New cemetery on Limestone Ridge
key_locations.details: Dry, wind-scoured burial ground above the floodline with fresh-cut tablets and straight rows.

key_locations.name: Marlowe Dam works
key_locations.details: Downriver site of blasting, trucks, payroll, and road-closure authority.

key_locations.name: Old road at church bend
key_locations.details: Road segment swallowed by the reservoir in chapter 18.

distances: The Bledsoe cabin is about half a mile uphill from Old Sion.
The temporary schoolhouse office is roughly one mile from the cemetery.
The Wolfe orchard lies about two miles downriver from the Bledsoe house.
The dam works are roughly five miles downstream and often audible from the valley.
Larkin is about nine miles away by road.
The new cemetery on Limestone Ridge is three miles above the old floodplain.
The church-bend road segment disappears under the reservoir by chapter 18.

world_rules: Federal compensation requires deed chain and death proof for deceased title holders.
County relocation accepts sworn local identification when stones or books fail, unless contested.
County-paid markers allow two lines plus supported dates; a third must be identification.
Unlicensed unions remain legally vulnerable in 1958 Tennessee even after years of cohabitation.
Illegitimate children have little inheritance standing without formal acknowledgment, legitimation, or a will.
Church record, county record, and lived family truth do not automatically match.

power_structure.mechanism: Condemnation deadlines, burial-move authority, and compensation release.
Affidavit recognition, contest windows, hearing schedules, and probate interpretation.

power_structure.holder: Marlowe Dam foremen and payroll office
power_structure.over: Local wage laborers and road access
power_structure.mechanism: Cash wages, shift assignments, road closures, and gate control.

power_structure.over: The Wolfe tract story and papers
power_structure.mechanism: Deed possession, church standing, and long control of the family narrative.
Memory, copied extracts, and firsthand burial knowledge.

objects.name: Midwife ledger
objects.owner: Della Bledsoe until the theft in chapter 08; current holder unknown.
objects.origin: Private notebook of births, stillbirths, unofficial marriages, and burial corrections the county never received.
objects.status: Reopened in chapter 02, selectively copied in chapter 08, stolen before chapter 09; never recovered.

objects.name: Gabriel Wolfe's pocketwatch
objects.owner: Originally Gabriel Wolfe; recovered by Della and then placed in county hearing review.
objects.origin: Personal effect left in the Wolfe orchard packing shed with older tract papers.
objects.status: Broken and initialed G.W.; found in chapter 11 and used to support Gabriel's identification.

objects.name: Wolfe parcel map
objects.owner: Recovered from the orchard and held for county hearing review.
objects.origin: Tract copy with Gabriel's share overpenciled under Vernon and note "N. Acker settled at marriage; G. absent, management consolidated."
objects.status: Found in chapter 11 and used in chapter 15 to reopen Noreen's claim.

objects.name: Rafe's false Eli affidavit
objects.owner: Filed by Rafe; county keeps the withdrawn record while he burns his copy.
objects.origin: Emergency filing meant to identify the fever-plot remains as Eli and unfreeze the Bledsoe claim.
objects.status: Revealed in chapter 09, marked withdrawn in chapter 14, and unsupported in chapter 15.

objects.name: Copied living-record pages
objects.owner: Della Bledsoe until chapter 18, then June Bledsoe.
objects.origin: Selective extracts Della makes once only grave- and claim-relevant facts can leave her memory.
objects.status: Passed to June in chapter 18; stored in a lard can while Gabriel's page stays with Della.

objects.name: Gabriel Wolfe marker
objects.owner: Placed over Gabriel Wolfe's grave on Limestone Ridge as a county relocation stone.
objects.origin: County limestone replacement marker ordered after Gabriel's reburial.
objects.status: Inscription: 'Gabriel Wolfe / Taken up from Old Sion fever ground / Reburied on Limestone Ridge, 1958.'

objects.name: June's cemetery notebook
objects.origin: School notebook June repurposes for marker orders and separating confirmed from uncertain facts.
objects.status: Started in chapter 17 and kept with the copied pages by chapter 18.

objects.name: Gabriel page
objects.origin: Separate page preserving Gabriel's burial facts and the false father entry under Rafe's birth.
objects.status: Kept in Della's apron pocket at the end of chapter 18.

debts.creditor: First State Bank of Hale County
debts.amount: $486 on truck note and feed advance
debts.deadline: Originally July 15, 1958; repeatedly extended under threat.
debts.status: Still delinquent after the Eli affidavit collapses, so no payout is released.
Still active; short dam wages slow collection without clearing it.

income_sources.amount: About $4 per day while the move lasts
income_sources.reliability: Reliable only until the cemetery move ends in chapter 16.
Unreliable because condemnation, weather, and lost ground keep shrinking the farm base.

income_sources.amount: About $1.35 per hour for mucking/truck guidance; better on powder crew and spillway cleanout
income_sources.reliability: Most reliable cash source, but tied to the project destroying the valley.
Low and vulnerable to gossip after chapter 07.

knowledge_state.knows: Gabriel Wolfe lies in the fever plot, fathered Rafe, and died before Eli fled.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: rafe_bledsoe, ruthie_bledsoe, june_bledsoe, lucille_holt, vernon_wolfe, noreen_acker

knowledge_state.knows: He helped bury Gabriel and knows the grave by placement, collarbone, and tooth.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: rafe_bledsoe, ruthie_bledsoe, june_bledsoe, lucille_holt, vernon_wolfe, noreen_acker

knowledge_state.knows: Della is withholding decisive truth about the fever-plot grave.
Gabriel is a dangerous family name tied to the cemetery and the Bledsoe house.
The central secret is about blood and naming, not just a misplaced marker.
Rafe's affidavit naming the remains as Eli is active unless challenged in time.
Recovered parcel evidence suggests her exclusion from the orchard was deliberate.
Gabriel fathered Rafe and Eli killed Gabriel before fleeing.
Gabriel Wolfe was his biological father and Eli Bledsoe was not.
The full paternity truth and why Gabriel's grave mattered.
Copied pages are only for grave- and claim-specific corrections; Gabriel's page stays with Della.

environmental_constants: Dam blasting arrives as recurring low concussion even out of sight.
Red clay clings to hems, boots, coffin boards, and ruts.
Rising water smells of algae, metal, drowned weeds, and rot.
Opened graves smell of damp earth, old wood, cloth rot, and fresh limestone.
Cicadas and late-summer insects press on outdoor scenes.
The schoolhouse office smells of chalk dust, wet paper, and bad coffee.
By early September the reservoir makes water-noises in roads and culverts.
